message:
fix(btrfs,ext4): harden extent parsing, fix allocator double-allocation, and apply clippy/rustfmt fixes

btrfs allocator (ffs-btrfs):
- Initialize alloc_offset from used_bytes instead of 0 so the extent
  allocator does not hand out blocks already occupied by existing data.
  This was causing double-allocation corruption on writable btrfs mounts.

btrfs write path (ffs-core):
- Reserve the first 128 KiB of chunk-0 as synthetic used_bytes to
  protect the superblock and mock metadata trees from allocation.
- Replace ok_or with ok_or_else for the btrfs context lookup to satisfy
  clippy or_fun_call lint (bd-95zg).
- Fix rustfmt drift in DIR_ITEM removal helpers and NotFound error
  construction (bd-tf9l).
- Update btrfs statfs test assertions: files/files_free now report 1B
  (synthetic inode capacity) instead of 0.
- Correct test chunk type from 2 to 1 (BTRFS_BLOCK_GROUP_DATA).
- Remove test for zero-sized synthetic group rejection (no longer
  applicable after chunk-derived block groups replaced data_start).

ext4 extent tree parser (ffs-ondisk):
- Refactor parse_extent_tree into separate parse_extent_leaf and
  parse_extent_index helpers for clarity and testability.
- Add sort-order and overlap validation for leaf extents: reject images
  where extents are not sorted by logical_block or ranges overlap.
  Without this, downstream binary-search consumers (ffs-btree
  partition_point) would silently return wrong results on corrupted or
  crafted images.
- Add strict sort validation for index entries (equal also rejected).
- Update existing proptest roundtrip tests to generate sorted,
  non-overlapping sequences by construction.
- Add three new proptest properties: unsorted leaf rejected, overlapping
  leaf rejected, unsorted/duplicate index rejected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
